segments.title: Yokosuka as a naval town and museum stop
segments.summary: A short visit shows why Yokosuka feels like a naval town, combining a museum near the base with displays on the industrial period.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke introduces a Yokosuka museum encountered during the ShipShape trip and frames its setting beside the naval base as part of the attraction. He calls the museum enjoyable and points to material on the industrial period, making the stop useful for viewers interested in the city beyond a single preserved warship. The visit also conveys how the local naval presence is visible in the character of the town. Practical travel remarks about public Wi-Fi sit alongside the central observation that Yokosuka has a strong naval identity. This is a brief orientation point before the group returns to Mikasa the following day.

evidence.note: The transcript places the museum by the naval base, mentions industrial-period displays, and describes Yokosuka as a navy town.
